symptoms: Angular water-soaked leaf spots that turn brown, wilting of leaves, gum exudation from stems, dieback of shoots.
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plant parts. Apply copper-based bactericides (copper hydroxide 2g/L). Use clean planting material from certified sources.
prevention: Use resistant varieties (TME 419, IITA varieties). Practice crop rotation with non-host crops. Avoid working in fields when foliage is wet.
organic_options: Neem oil spray (5ml/L), compost tea applications to boost plant immunity, biological control agents like Bacillus subtilis.

symptoms: Yellow chlorotic patches on mature leaves, brown streaks on green stems, root necrosis with brown dry rot inside tuberous roots.
treatment: No curative treatment available. Remove and burn all infected plants immediately. Replant with virus-free certified cuttings.
prevention: Use CBSD-tolerant varieties. Control whitefly vectors (Bemisia tabaci) with yellow sticky traps. Source clean planting material.
organic_options: Neem-based insecticides for whitefly control. Intercrop with repellent plants like marigold. Maintain field hygiene.

symptoms: Green mottling pattern on leaves, mild leaf distortion, mosaic-like patterns with varying shades of green.
treatment: Remove symptomatic plants to reduce inoculum source. Use healthy stem cuttings for new plantings.
prevention: Use virus-free planting material. Control insect vectors. Practice field sanitation.
organic_options: Maintain healthy soil with organic compost. Encourage beneficial insects that prey on aphid and whitefly vectors.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are uniformly green with normal shape and size.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management practices.
prevention: Maintain good agricultural practices: balanced fertilization, proper spacing, regular weed control, and crop monitoring.
organic_options: Continue organic mulching, compost applications, and companion planting to maintain plant health.

symptoms: Chlorotic yellow/white mosaic pattern on leaves, leaf curling and distortion, reduced leaf size, stunted growth.
treatment: Rogue out infected plants immediately. No chemical cure exists. Replace with CMD-resistant varieties.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties (TME 3, TMS 30572). Control Bemisia tabaci whitefly vectors. Use certified virus-free cuttings.
organic_options: Whitefly control using neem oil, yellow sticky traps, and biological agents like Encarsia formosa parasitoid wasp.

symptoms: Small, round to elongated brown-red pustules (uredinia) on both leaf surfaces, scattered across the leaf blade.
treatment: Apply fungicides at early onset: Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) or Propiconazole 25EC (1ml/L). Apply at 10-14 day intervals.
prevention: Plant rust-resistant hybrids. Early planting to avoid peak spore dispersal. Maintain balanced nitrogen fertilization.
organic_options: Sulfur-based fungicides (approved organic). Baking soda spray (1 tbsp/gallon). Remove crop debris after harvest.

symptoms: Rectangular, gray-tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins, lesions become gray with distinct parallel edges.
treatment: Apply strobilurin-based fungicides (Azoxystrobin 250g/L at 1ml/L) at VT-R1 stage. Rotate with triazoles.
prevention: Crop rotation with non-host crops (2-year minimum). Tillage to bury infected residue. Use resistant hybrids.
organic_options: Deep tillage to bury residue. Wider row spacing for air circulation. Biological fungicides (Bacillus amyloliquefaciens).

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are uniformly green with normal venation pattern.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management.
prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization (N-P-K), proper irrigation scheduling, scouting for early disease detection.
organic_options: Continue cover cropping, compost applications, and beneficial insect habitat maintenance.

symptoms: Long, elliptical gray-green to tan lesions (1-6 inches), cigar-shaped, starting on lower leaves and moving upward.
treatment: Foliar fungicides: Azoxystrobin + Propiconazole at VT stage. Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) as protectant.
prevention: Plant resistant hybrids with Ht genes. Crop rotation (minimum 1 year). Tillage to decompose residue.
organic_options: Copper-based sprays (approved organic). Crop residue management. Trichoderma-based biocontrol agents.

symptoms: Dark brown-black concentric rings (target-board pattern) on older lower leaves, lesions may coalesce, premature leaf drop.
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil 75WP (2g/L) or Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) at first sign. Alternate with systemic fungicides.
prevention: Crop rotation (3-year minimum). Adequate fertilization and irrigation. Remove volunteer plants and crop debris.
organic_options: Copper hydroxide sprays. Bacillus subtilis biological fungicide. Composted mulch to prevent soil splash.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Foliage is uniformly green and vigorous.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management.
prevention: Use certified seed potatoes. Maintain proper hilling, irrigation, and phosphorus/potassium fertilization.
organic_options: Continue organic mulching, compost tea foliar applications, and companion planting with beans or marigold.

symptoms: Water-soaked pale green patches on leaf tips/edges turning dark brown-black, white mold growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions.
treatment: Immediate application of Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold MZ) 2.5g/L. Spray every 5-7 days during outbreak.
prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed. Use resistant varieties. Destroy cull piles. Monitor weather for blight-favorable conditions.
organic_options: Copper-based fungicides (early, preventive only). Remove and destroy infected foliage. Improve air circulation.

symptoms: Oval brown spots with gray centers on leaves, dark brown margin, may also appear on glumes causing grain discoloration.
treatment: Seed treatment with Carbendazim 50WP (2g/kg seed). Foliar spray of Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) or Propiconazole (1ml/L).
prevention: Use resistant varieties. Balanced fertilization (especially potassium and silica). Treat seeds before sowing.
organic_options: Pseudomonas fluorescens seed treatment. Trichoderma viride (4g/kg seed). Silica amendments from rice husk ash.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are uniformly green with normal tillering pattern.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular paddy management.
prevention: Maintain proper water management (alternate wetting/drying). Balanced N-P-K with silica. Regular field scouting.
organic_options: Green manure incorporation (Sesbania, Azolla). Organic compost. Neem cake application.

symptoms: White parallel streaks/mines on upper leaf surface caused by larval tunneling, scraping of green tissue, leaves appear whitish.
treatment: Spray Chlorpyrifos 20EC (2ml/L) or Quinalphos 25EC (2ml/L) for severe infestations. Clip affected leaf tips.
prevention: Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Remove grassy weeds from bunds. Maintain clean field borders.
organic_options: Hand-pick adults and egg masses. Neem oil spray (5ml/L). Encourage natural predators (spiders, dragonflies).

symptoms: Diamond/spindle-shaped gray-center spots with brown borders on leaves, lesions may coalesce causing leaf death.
treatment: Spray Tricyclazole 75WP (0.6g/L) or Isoprothiolane 40EC (1.5ml/L). Apply at first symptom appearance.
prevention: Use blast-resistant varieties. Avoid excess nitrogen. Split nitrogen applications. Proper water management.
organic_options: Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray. Silicon-based amendments. Trichoderma harzianum applications.

symptoms: Grayish-brown lesion at panicle base (neck node), panicle may break and cause whiteheads/chaffy grains, severe yield loss.
treatment: Preventive spray of Tricyclazole 75WP (0.6g/L) at booting stage. Carbendazim 50WP (1g/L) at flowering.
prevention: Avoid late-season nitrogen. Plant resistant varieties. Timely sowing to escape peak infection period.
organic_options: Bacillus subtilis spray at booting/heading. Silica fertilization. Avoid dense planting.

symptoms: White to cream-colored stripes along leaf veins, leaves may wilt from tip downward, vascular bundles show red discoloration.
treatment: No effective chemical cure. Remove and destroy infected stools. Disinfect cutting tools with 1% sodium hypochlorite.
prevention: Use disease-free seed cane from certified nurseries. Hot water treatment (50°C for 2 hours). Resistant varieties.
organic_options: Trichoderma-enriched farmyard manure. Crop residue burning. Bio-agents (Pseudomonas fluorescens) in soil.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are uniformly green with vigorous growth.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management.
prevention: Maintain proper nutrition (N-P-K + micronutrients). Adequate irrigation. Regular earthing-up and detrashing.
organic_options: Continue press-mud application, green manuring, and composting of sugarcane trash.

symptoms: Yellowing and drying of top leaves (crown region), red discoloration inside the cane stalk with white patches, alcohol smell from split cane.
treatment: No effective curative treatment. Uproot and destroy infected clumps. Avoid ratooning of infected fields.
prevention: Plant resistant varieties (Co 86032, CoC 671). Hot water treatment of setts (50°C, 2 hrs). Crop rotation.
organic_options: Trichoderma viride (4g/kg sett) sett treatment. Pseudomonas fluorescens. Organic amendments to improve soil health.

symptoms: Narrow dark-red to maroon stripes running parallel to midrib on leaf blade, merging to form larger necrotic areas.
treatment: Spray Streptocycline (500ppm) + Copper oxychloride (3g/L). Remove severely infected leaves.
prevention: Avoid excess nitrogen. Use resistant varieties. Proper spacing for air circulation. Drain waterlogged fields.
organic_options: Copper-based organic sprays. Maintain optimal nutrition with organic manures. Field sanitation.

symptoms: Elongated orange-brown pustules on lower leaf surface, yellow-orange spore masses, severe infection causes leaf drying.
treatment: Foliar spray of Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) or Propiconazole 25EC (1ml/L). Repeat at 15-day intervals.
prevention: Plant rust-resistant varieties. Remove and burn heavily infected leaves. Avoid late planting.
organic_options: Sulfur-based organic fungicides. Neem oil spray. Remove infected debris from field.

symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that become angular and scabby. Fruit spots are raised and rough-textured.
treatment: Copper hydroxide (2g/L) + Mancozeb (2.5g/L) sprays every 7-10 days. Streptomycin sulfate for severe cases.
prevention: Use certified disease-free seed/transplants. Avoid overhead irrigation. Crop rotation (2-3 years).
organic_options: Copper-based organic sprays. Bacillus amyloliquefaciens biological control. Proper staking for air circulation.

symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target-board lesions) on lower/older leaves, yellowing around lesions, premature defoliation.
treatment: Chlorothalonil 75WP (2g/L) or Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L). Alternate with Azoxystrobin for resistance management.
prevention: Stake plants for air movement. Mulch to prevent soil splash. Remove lower infected leaves. Crop rotation.
organic_options: Copper sprays. Compost tea. Trichoderma harzianum soil drench. Adequate calcium nutrition.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are dark green, compound, with normal serrated edges.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management and monitoring.
prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization (Ca, Mg important). Consistent watering schedule. Regular scouting.
organic_options: Continue compost applications, mulching, companion planting (basil, marigold), and beneficial insect habitat.

symptoms: Large, irregular water-soaked patches on leaves turning dark brown/black. White mold on leaf undersides. Firm brown rot on fruit.
treatment: Immediate spray of Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold MZ) 2.5g/L. Repeat every 5-7 days in wet weather.
prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation. Provide adequate spacing. Use resistant varieties. Destroy crop debris.
organic_options: Copper fungicides (preventive only). Remove and destroy infected tissue immediately. Improve drainage.

symptoms: Yellow-green patches on upper leaf surface, olive-green to brown velvety mold on corresponding underside (greenhouse/humid conditions).
treatment: Apply Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) or Chlorothalonil (2g/L). Improve greenhouse ventilation.
prevention: Increase air circulation (ventilation, proper spacing). Reduce humidity below 85%. Avoid leaf wetness.
organic_options: Potassium bicarbonate spray. Bacillus subtilis. Proper pruning for airflow. Drip irrigation only.

symptoms: Light and dark green mottling/mosaic on leaves, leaf distortion (fern-leaf), stunted growth, fruit may show internal browning.
treatment: No curable treatment once infected. Remove and destroy infected plants. Do not save seed from infected plants.
prevention: Use virus-free seed and resistant varieties (Tm-2 gene). Disinfect tools with 10% trisodium phosphate. Wash hands with milk.
organic_options: Milk spray (1:9 ratio with water) as preventive. Avoid tobacco use near tomato plants. Control aphid vectors.

symptoms: Small circular spots (1-3mm) with dark brown margins and gray-white centers with tiny dark dots (pycnidia), starting on lower leaves.
treatment: Mancozeb 75WP (2.5g/L) or Chlorothalonil (2g/L) sprays at 7-10 day intervals. Remove infected lower leaves.
prevention: Mulch around base to prevent splash. Stake plants. Crop rotation (minimum 2 years). Avoid overhead watering.
organic_options: Copper-based organic fungicides. Neem oil. Remove and compost (hot composting) infected debris.

symptoms: Fine stippling/bronzing on leaf upper surface, fine webbing on undersides, leaves turn yellow-brown and dry up in severe cases.
treatment: Spray Abamectin 1.8EC (0.5ml/L) or Spiromesifen 240SC (0.8ml/L). Strong water spray to dislodge mites.
prevention: Avoid drought stress (adequate irrigation). Monitor underside of leaves regularly. Avoid excess nitrogen.
organic_options: Neem oil spray (5ml/L). Insecticidal soap. Predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis). Strong water spray.

symptoms: Brown circular lesions with concentric rings on leaves/stems/fruit, lesions may coalesce causing defoliation.
treatment: Chlorothalonil (2g/L) or Azoxystrobin (1ml/L) sprays. Begin at first sign of disease.
prevention: Good air circulation. Avoid wetting foliage. Remove lower affected leaves. Crop rotation.
organic_options: Copper hydroxide sprays. Bacillus subtilis. Proper pruning and staking. Organic mulch.

symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaf margins, stunted internodes, small leaves, reduced fruit set, plant appears bushy.
treatment: No curative treatment. Remove and destroy infected plants to reduce virus reservoir. Control whitefly vectors.
prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties (Ty-1, Ty-3 genes). Whitefly control with reflective mulches and netting.
organic_options: Yellow sticky traps for whitefly. Neem oil. Reflective aluminum mulch. Parasitoid wasps (Eretmocerus).

symptoms: Small, round orange-brown pustules scattered randomly on upper leaf surface, rupturing epidermis to release spores.
treatment: Propiconazole 25EC (1ml/L) or Tebuconazole 250EC (1ml/L) at first appearance. Repeat after 15 days if needed.
prevention: Grow resistant varieties (Lr genes). Timely sowing (avoid late planting). Balanced nitrogen application.
organic_options: Sulfur-based fungicides. Remove alternate hosts (Thalictrum species). Crop residue management.

symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Leaves are uniformly green with normal tillering and vigor.
treatment: No treatment necessary. Continue regular crop management.
prevention: Timely sowing, balanced fertilization, proper irrigation scheduling, and regular disease monitoring.
organic_options: Continue organic manure applications, green manuring, and maintaining soil biodiversity.

symptoms: Irregular tan-brown blotches with dark borders on leaves, tiny black dots (pycnidia) visible within lesions, starting from lower leaves.
treatment: Spray Propiconazole 25EC (1ml/L) at GS 32-39. Alternate with Azoxystrobin for resistance management.
prevention: Crop rotation. Resistant cultivars. Seedbed hygiene (incorporate residue). Avoid excess nitrogen.
organic_options: Copper-based sprays. Deep plowing to bury infected stubble. Biological agents (Trichoderma).

symptoms: Bright yellow-orange pustules arranged in stripes along leaf veins, giving striped appearance. Severe infection kills leaves.
treatment: Immediate spray of Propiconazole 25EC (1ml/L) or Tebuconazole (1ml/L). Two sprays at 15-day intervals.
prevention: Plant Yr-gene resistant varieties. Monitor during cool-wet weather. Avoid susceptible late-sown crops.
organic_options: Sulfur sprays (early preventive). Remove volunteer/self-sown wheat. Maintain balanced plant nutrition.
